name: ReFi Colombia
hub: ReFi Colombia (national coordinator of regional ReFi nodes)
leads: Ximena Monclou, with WairaMoon, 0xJ4an and Tereza Bizkova
place: Rainforest, coastline and mountain city across Colombia's bioregions
tagline: Regeneration and a first wallet, from the Amazon to the coast
whyItMattered: Colombia is the second most biodiverse country on Earth, yet many communities don't feel the urgency of protecting it. ReFi Colombia tried to make stewardship tangible by pairing hands-on ecological work with on-chain tools that put real money and verifiable participation in local hands.
story: The $7,840 grant landed on-chain on 9 December 2025. The plan was ambitious: scale a subsidy program from 50 to 250 people, fund five nodes, and run an English course. Reality was smaller and more modest.

ReFi Colombia opened a mini-grants round for its nodes. Only two applied, Cartagena and Amazonas, and both were funded. Their first applications were incomplete, so the team held a meeting to walk through expectations, metrics and reporting before releasing funds.

Cartagena ran five activities and Amazonas four, weaving mangrove and native-tree planting, urban gardens and circular-economy workshops together with first wallets and POAPs. Weather and a safety incident forced postponements; the team adapted each time.

Today most of the grant, roughly $4,250, is still held in the treasury. The English course remains in preparation, and the flagship subsidy program, though genuinely live, predates this grant and barely grew.

signatureStory.title: Regenerative Roots: planting in the Amazon after the flood
signatureStory.text: ReFi Amazonas planned a community tree-planting at the CercaViva Reserve, 11 km from Leticia, with 44 people registered. Heavy rain flooded the streets on the Friday; by Saturday travel was unsafe and the leader, worried for participants, postponed to the following week. When it finally happened, about half returned. The Environmental Police Brigade joined, native species went into the ground, and a session on composting and soil care followed. Nearly everyone present, 98%, downloaded a wallet and minted their first POAP. The day closed with a communal meal of Casabe, the traditional cassava dish, tying technology back to ancestral roots.
signatureStory.quote: 98% downloaded their first wallet and minted their first POAP as a record of participation.

delivered: Opened a 2-node mini-grants round and funded both applicants (Cartagena and Amazonas), ~$2,280 disbursed on-chain on 2026-02-03
Cartagena ran 5 activities (including mangrove reforestation); Amazonas ran 4 (native-tree planting, urban pollinator garden, blockchain-over-coffee conversations)
Kept the ReFi Medellin subsidy smart contract live, with a public Dune dashboard and TuCOP wallet claiming
Used POAPs and wallets for event onboarding across nodes (9 POAP drops; 26 new wallets reported)
Maintained on-chain transparency via a public Safe and dashboard as promised
English course not launched; ~$4,250-4,700 of the grant still held in the treasury

outcomes.text: Two local nodes strengthened their capacity to run regenerative activities and onboard communities to Web3, with real cross-node collaboration
The subsidy program kept supporting low-income participants with weekly payments, but it predates the grant and grew only modestly (~50 to 65-78)
Stronger alliances with local youth and environmental organisations, and demand for the still-pending English course

status: Continuing; round ongoing, most of the grant still to be deployed

verdict.line: A real, on-chain-verifiable program from a proven team whose financials largely hold up, with one genuine defect ($7,000 of pre-grant co-funding double-counted as spend) and low additionality.

honesty: The money moves cleanly on-chain and is fully accounted for, but the report claims more scale than the numbers support: a $7,000 pre-grant co-funding deposit is double-counted as spend, the flagship subsidy program predates the grant, the application's $5k-cash-plus-$5k-in-kind co-funding quietly became a flat $7,000 with the in-kind absent, and order-of-magnitude target misses (250 to ~78 beneficiaries, 5 to 2 nodes, no English course) went undisclosed.

proposed.summary: Run three programs scaling regenerative activity and Web3 onboarding across Colombia's bioregions via 5 regional nodes.
points: Scale the transferred ReFi Medellin subsidy contract from 50 to 250 beneficiaries
Fund 5 local nodes at $1,500-2,500 each via multisig transfers
Run a 4-month English course onboarding 15-20 participants to Web3
Hit 500+ participants, 450+ new wallets, 500+ trees, 30%+ women

correlation.proposed: Scale subsidy contract from 50 to 250 beneficiaries
correlation.outcome: Subsidy program stayed live but grew only to ~65-78 recipients (roughly flat) and predates the grant.
Only Cartagena and Amazonas applied; both funded, ~$2,280 disbursed on-chain on 2026-02-03. 3 of 5 nodes inactive.

correlation.proposed: Run 4-month English course onboarding 15-20 to Web3
correlation.outcome: Not launched; reported as still in preparation at submission.
Far below target: ~161 participants and 26 new wallets reported across node events.
Real ecological work (mangrove, native-tree planting, pollinator garden) but no measured tree/hectare/gender outcomes given.

correlation.proposed: Maintain on-chain transparency via public Safe and Dune
correlation.outcome: Public Safe and live Dune dashboard maintained; both token ledgers reconcile to the penny on-chain.

team.name: WairaMoon
team.role: ReFi Colombia lead, social media and design (Amazonas)

team.name: Ximena Monclou
team.role: Legal advisor; led the grant request and oversight (Medellin)

funding.matchNote: Verified on-chain, but deposited before the grant arrived and double-counted as program spend in the report; the committed $5k cash + $5k in-kind became a flat $7,000 with the in-kind absent.

disbursements.purpose: Local node grant for 5 community activities
Local node grant for 4 community activities
Ops and coordination, including a transfer to the legal lead's wallet
Social-media work (first instalment of a $150-of-$600 commitment)

stories.title: A round where everyone who applied got funded
stories.text: ReFi Colombia opened a mini-grants round for its five nodes, but only Cartagena and Amazonas applied. Their first applications were incomplete, so the team met with both to walk through expectations, metrics and reporting before releasing roughly $2,280 on-chain on 3 February 2026. Three of five nodes were inactive; Medellin opted out.
stories.quote: In the end, only two nodes participated: ReFi Cartagena and ReFi Amazonas. ReFi Medellin decided not to participate because they felt the funds could be more useful for nodes that needed them more…

stories.title: Planting in the Amazon, after the flood
stories.text: ReFi Amazonas planned a tree-planting at the CercaViva Reserve, 11 km from Leticia, with 44 registered. Heavy rain flooded the streets, so the leader postponed a week for safety. About half returned. The Environmental Police Brigade joined, native species went into the ground, and the day closed with Casabe, the traditional cassava dish.
stories.quote: 98% downloaded their first wallet and minted their first POAP as a record of participation.

stories.title: The flagship that predated the grant
stories.text: The report foregrounds a live subsidy program paying weekly support through a Celo smart contract. It is genuinely active, but on-chain records show it ran since December 2024 and was fed by the $7,000 co-funding deposited before the grant arrived. Recipients grew only modestly, from about 50 to 65-78, well short of the 250 target.

dimensionEvidence.evidence: Deliberate, multi-event ecological focus: mangrove planting in Cartagena, native-tree planting through Regenerative Roots in the Amazon, and an urban pollinator garden. Real hands-on stewardship, though no measured outcomes (trees survived, hectares, biodiversity sites) were reported.

Cross-node activity and community events brought participants from Cartagena, Amazonas and Medellin together, and youth-organisation gatherings (the blockchain-over-coffee conversations) built new alliances. Reasonably evidenced via recaps and POAPs, though claims of durable networks are asserted rather than shown.

POAPs for attendance and the TuCOP wallet with cUSD/COPm on Celo for subsidies were genuinely used and verified on-chain, providing transparency and Web3 onboarding (26 new wallets reported). A well-calibrated 'Meaningful' role, with no local technical capacity built to maintain the systems independently.

timeline.what: Celo Colombia and ReFi Medellin co-funding deposited on-chain
Node grants disbursed to Cartagena and Amazonas
Regenerative Roots tree planting in the Amazon
Cartagena and Amazonas ran regeneration events

fundingNote: Of the $7,840 grant, only ~$2,280 reached the two nodes; roughly $4,250-4,700 (over half) remains undeployed in the Safe. The list below shows itemised, on-chain-verifiable outflows; the report's own totals run slightly higher (e.g. $690 claimed for tools vs the $80 Canva row verified, $150 claimed paid to social media vs the $49 instalment verified).
